famous quote:
"Do not be deceived, Wormwood. Our cause is never more in danger than when a human, no longer desiring, but still intending, to do our Enemy's will, looks round upon a universe from which every trace of Him seems to have vanished, and asks why he has been forsaken, and still obeys."
-- C.S. Lewis (The Screwtape Letters)
Interview Text:
Mormon Missionaries:
 (I figure many religions talk to and hear from God, so I wanted to learn from them)

Some Mormon missionaries came by my door and I met with them a few times. I asked them how they hear the spirit, and how they know it is the spirit. 

Elder Pachella said that sometimes you don't even know it's the spirit. Before he was on his mission he felt compelled to do things but it wasn't until he came on his mission that he realized it was God speaking to him. If you're prompted to do something good it's from God. If it's evil it's from the devil. 

He said, "We've been taught to be first responders." If you think of something good to do, don't think any more about it. Just do it. Don't question the spirit.

One day he was having a very bad day because he was seriously questioning his faith. But before they were supposed to go out and tract he saw his scriptures setting there and he sat down to read it. In the Doctrine and the Covenants, section 6, Joseph Smith's scribe asks God if it's all true and if he should really be on this mission. It says, [check source]"I've told you to come to this place at this time because you follow my commandments. That is why you are here right now. I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God. I have given you the answer. Elder Pachella started crying. It had answered him as if God had answered him himself. 

Elder Keller says it is when a thought jumps into his head. Sometimes he'll pass it up. But his missionary companion will call him out and help him follow through with it. The little things matter the most, not the big fancy things. "I feel the spirit like a super calm." He will feel it sometimes when reading the scriptures and other church documents. 

A week or so later I saw some Mormon missionaries in a grocery store parking lot. I took a double take to see if they were my new Mormon friends. 

They weren't but while I watched a woman walked by carrying a heavy pack of bottled water. The missionaries took a double take and triple take and I could see them discussing whether they should help her. I imagine I was witnessing them listening to the spirit.
Music Video Instructions: Though people let you down, have faith in God.
